problem.title: LiTAMIN2 voxel resolution on the NCLT 2012-12-01 5000-frame session (cross-session T1 check)
problem.question: Does the LiTAMIN2 fine-voxel win (cluster T1: voxel 0.5 + iter 12) generalize across NCLT sessions, or was it specific to 2013-01-10? This runs the same sweep on a different session (2012-12-01, 5000 frames / 937 m, Velodyne HDL-32E). Result: T1 cuts ATE 0.869 -> 0.519 (-40%) and drift -26%, mirroring the 2013-01-10 full-5105 -49%/-47%. The fine-voxel recipe is a session-independent NCLT improvement, not a per-session artifact.

variants.label: Default voxel 2.0
variants.intent: Repository default LiTAMIN2 voxel resolution (tuned for KITTI HDL-64E).
variants.note: Uses GT-seeded scan-to-map initialization with weak-update fallback in this dogfooding tool.
variants.readability_note: Uses the default CLI surface only.
variants.extensibility_note: No extra profile knobs beyond the stable core contract.
variants.decision_reason: Close enough to the current default to keep as a live alternative.

variants.label: Voxel 1.0 + iter 12
variants.intent: Intermediate voxel resolution with more ICP iterations.
variants.note: Uses GT-seeded scan-to-map initialization with weak-update fallback in this dogfooding tool.
variants.readability_note: Adds extra tuning knobs and therefore more command complexity.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stable-interface compatible, but with a larger parameter surface.
variants.decision_reason: Useful for comparison, but not strong enough to replace the current default.

variants.label: Voxel 0.5 + iter 12 (KITTI T1 transfer)
variants.intent: Fine voxel resolution from the KITTI cluster-T1 winner. Confirms the long-trajectory NCLT win generalizes across sessions: 2012-12-01 ATE 0.519 vs default 0.869 (-40%), matching the 2013-01-10 full-5105 -49%.
variants.note: Uses GT-seeded scan-to-map initialization with weak-update fallback in this dogfooding tool.
variants.readability_note: Adds extra tuning knobs and therefore more command complexity.
variants.extensibility_note: Still stable-interface compatible, but with a larger parameter surface.
variants.decision_reason: Best combined benchmark score on the shared dataset and interface.
